comment_text:
Everyone already knows the story and has realized it couldn’t be true, so Netflix wasn’t going to spend an hour and a half just repeating what everyone knows. They needed to create conflict, and that’s what Netflix does best, stirring up debate, not delivering quality or ethical accuracy. It’s all about viewership. That’s why they even renewed Meghan Markle’s program, despite her becoming one of the most disliked people on social media in 2025. Netflix knows people will still watch to criticize, even if the show is voted the worst ever.

The same applies here: it’s not about what’s true or false, but about creating controversy. That’s why Netflix focuses on irrelevant conflicts with the Chicago police instead of addressing the obvious holes in Jussie’s story. But it won’t work because people have already made up their minds, and this documentary won’t change that.

Look at the Menendez brothers case: even when the Netflix documentary clearly showed they murdered their parents purely for financial gain and that they fabricated the sexual abuse claims, there were still people who believed they were justified because of those alleged accusations. So naturally, there will also be people who choose to believe Jussie Smollett, no matter how obvious the truth is.

comment_text:
Having watched the documentary,  and really the first time of hearing about it.
1. There is no firm evidence that he wa attacked by anyone, no CCTV footage, so was he even fake attacked?
2. Surveillance of nearby CCTV and investigation into taxi and car share, demonstrates that the brothers were at least in the area.
3. The two people who say they saw a white man, again cannot place the person at the place of the attack, just the surrounding area.
4. I am willing to heavily suspect that a white friend or 2, struggling actor, was also paid to be in the area at the time of the attack.
5. Video Surveillance inside the building does not demonstrate a person who has been attacked, not only does he have his Subway Sandwich, but he also still wears the rope, and there is no sign of fear.
6. His seeming reluctance on the 999 call made by whoever, to report the crime, suggests that he didn't want it to be criminally investigated, but rather a social media video (if the attack did happen)
7. Even when shown the video of a supposed white guy by the taxi, many identified as black, so no hard evidence either way.

Remember it happened on a dark snowy cold night when colours are altered due to streetlights, moon and snow reflection.

I would be prepared to say that at the very least it did not happen as he said, and there is more evidence towards hoax than to real, but as there is no video of the actual attack the documentary does not prove either way with 100% clarity.
